filters.label: Shipped to production
filters.description: An application or workflow was deployed to a live production environment, maintained, or integrated into an active operational system.
filters.job_description_source: Own the full build lifecycle: prototype in the AI Lab, productionize through Internal Transformation, and maintain solutions post-deployment...
filters.extraction_rule: Look for explicit confirmation that an application or workflow was deployed to an end-user production environment, maintained live, or integrated into an active operational system. Do not trip for sandboxes, local scripts, or un-deployed prototypes.

filters.label: Governance / risk mitigation
filters.description: Treats risk, governance, ethics, security, privacy, or trust as a deliberate design constraint.
filters.job_description_source: treating risk, governance, ethics, and trust as core design constraints rather than afterthoughts.
filters.extraction_rule: Scan for explicit mentions of security boundary design, data privacy restrictions, compliance/regulatory frameworks, or automated evaluation guardrails (e.g., hallucination monitoring, PII redacting, or human-in-the-loop vetoes).

filters.label: Multi-step workflow design
filters.description: Architecture beyond a single prompt-and-response: orchestration, agent routing, human-AI handoffs, or decision boundaries.
filters.job_description_source: You think in workflows, not just models, designing multi-step processes that account for human-AI handoffs, decision boundaries, and appropriate levels of autonomy.
filters.extraction_rule: The text must detail an architecture that uses more than a single prompt-and-response model call. It must explicitly cite multi-step agent routing, orchestrations, distinct human-AI handoff thresholds, or programmatic decision trees.

filters.label: Non-traditional / low-code delivery
filters.description: Built operational automation or agentic behavior via iPaaS, low-code, API stitching, or config-driven orchestration rather than raw code.
filters.job_description_source: ...whether through code, configuration, low-code platforms, APIs, or orchestration tools. We know the strongest builders come from a wide range of backgrounds, including engineering, business, strategy, design, and self-taught paths.
filters.extraction_rule: Mark as true if the candidate explicitly built operational automation or agentic behaviors using alternative methods such as iPaaS (e.g., Make, Zapier), low-code enterprise modules, API stitching, or config-driven orchestration tools, rather than standard raw code repositories.

filters.label: Concrete technical tradeoffs
filters.description: States an explicit engineering or design compromise and what was chosen.
filters.job_description_source: What was the hardest decision you faced, and what did you decide? / You can make reasonable assumptions, build quickly, test, learn, and iterate.
filters.extraction_rule: Look for the explicit presence of an engineering or design compromise (e.g., prioritizing execution speed over model size, choosing deterministic code over dynamic LLM generations, or managing API cost limits).
